list.description: Lists information about the supported locations for this service. This method lists locations based on the resource scope provided in the ListLocationsRequest.name field: * **Global locations**: If `name` is empty, the method lists the public locations available to all projects. * **Project-specific locations**: If `name` follows the format `projects/{project}`, the method lists locations visible to that specific project. This includes public, private, or other project-specific locations enabled for the project. For gRPC and client library implementations, the resource name is passed as the `name` field. For direct service calls, the resource name is incorporated into the request path based on the specific service implementation and version.

fetchStorageInventory.description: List remote source's inventory of storage resources. The remote source is another cloud vendor (e.g. AWS, Azure). The inventory describes the list of existing storage resources in that source. Note that this operation lists the resources on the remote source, as opposed to listing the MigratingVms resources in the vmmigration service.

create.description: Creates a new Source in a given project and location.

requestId.description: A request ID to identify requests. Specify a unique request ID so that if you must retry your request, the server will know to ignore the request if it has already been completed. The server will guarantee that for at least 60 minutes since the first request. For example, consider a situation where you make an initial request and the request times out. If you make the request again with the same request ID, the server can check if original operation with the same request ID was received, and if so, will ignore the second request. This prevents clients from accidentally creating duplicate commitments. The request ID must be a valid UUID with the exception that zero UUID is not supported (00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000).

parent.description: Required. The Source's parent.

fetchInventory.description: List remote source's inventory of VMs. The remote source is the onprem vCenter (remote in the sense it's not in Compute Engine). The inventory describes the list of existing VMs in that source. Note that this operation lists the VMs on the remote source, as opposed to listing the MigratingVms resources in the vmmigration service.

pauseMigration.description: Pauses a migration for a VM. If cycle tasks are running they will be cancelled, preserving source task data. Further replication cycles will not be triggered while the VM is paused.

startMigration.description: Starts migration for a VM. Starts the process of uploading data and creating snapshots, in replication cycles scheduled by the policy.

resumeMigration.description: Resumes a migration for a VM. When called on a paused migration, will start the process of uploading data and creating snapshots; when called on a completed cut-over migration, will update the migration to active state and start the process of uploading data and creating snapshots.

CloneJob.description: CloneJob describes the process of creating a clone of a MigratingVM to the requested target based on the latest successful uploaded snapshots. While the migration cycles of a MigratingVm take place, it is possible to verify the uploaded VM can be started in the cloud, by creating a clone. The clone can be created without any downtime, and it is created using the latest snapshots which are already in the cloud. The cloneJob is only responsible for its work, not its products, which means once it is finished, it will never touch the instance it created. It will only delete it in case of the CloneJob being cancelled or upon failure to clone.

If not specified, the server chooses a default device name to apply to this disk, in the form persistent-disk-x, where x is a number assigned by Google Compute Engine. This field is only applicable for persistent disks.

BootDiskDefaults.description: BootDiskDefaults hold information about the boot disk of a VM.

name.description: Output only. The Source name.

encryption.description: Optional. Immutable. The encryption details of the source data stored by the service.

createTime.description: Output only. The create time timestamp.

error.description: Output only. Provides details on the state of the Source in case of an error.

updateTime.description: Output only. The update time timestamp.

Source.description: Source message describes a specific vm migration Source resource. It contains the source environment information.

ComputeEngineTargetDefaults.description: ComputeEngineTargetDefaults is a collection of details for creating a VM in a target Compute Engine project.

diskReplicaZones.description: Optional. Additional replica zones of the target regional disks. If this list is not empty a regional disk will be created. The first supported zone would be the one stated in the zone field. The rest are taken from this list. Please refer to the [regional disk creation API](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/regions-zones/global-regional-zonal-resources) for further details about regional vs zonal disks. If not specified, a zonal disk will be created in the same zone the VM is created.

hostname.description: The hostname to assign to the VM.

bootConversion.description: Optional. By default the virtual machine will keep its existing boot option. Setting this property will trigger an internal process which will convert the virtual machine from using the existing boot option to another.
enumDescriptions: Unspecified conversion type.
No conversion.
Convert from BIOS to EFI.

serviceAccount.description: Optional. The service account to associate the VM with.

metadata.description: The metadata key/value pairs to assign to the VM.

secureBoot.description: Defines whether the instance has Secure Boot enabled. This can be set to true only if the VM boot option is EFI.

labels.description: A map of labels to associate with the VM.

storagePool.description: Optional. If specified this will be the storage pool in which the disk is created. This is the full path of the storage pool resource, for example: "projects/my-project/zones/us-central1-a/storagePools/my-storage-pool". The storage pool must be in the same project and zone as the target disks. The storage pool's type must match the disk type.

appliedLicense.description: Output only. The OS license returned from the adaptation module report.

encryption.description: Optional. Immutable. The encryption to apply to the VM disks.

zone.description: The zone in which to create the VM.

targetProject.description: The full path of the resource of type TargetProject which represents the Compute Engine project in which to create this VM.

enableIntegrityMonitoring.description: Optional. Defines whether the instance has integrity monitoring enabled. This can be set to true only if the VM boot option is EFI, and vTPM is enabled.

additionalLicenses.description: Additional licenses to assign to the VM.

enumDescriptions: The boot option is unknown.
The boot option is EFI.
The boot option is BIOS.
bootOption.description: Output only. The VM Boot Option, as set in the source VM.

adaptationModifiers.description: Optional. AdaptationModifiers are the set of modifiers used during OS adaptation.

enableVtpm.description: Optional. Defines whether the instance has vTPM enabled. This can be set to true only if the VM boot option is EFI.

computeScheduling.description: Compute instance scheduling information (if empty default is used).

networkInterfaces.description: List of NICs connected to this VM.

machineType.description: The machine type to create the VM with.

enumDescriptions: The license type is the default for the OS.
The license type is Pay As You Go license type.
The license type is Bring Your Own License type.
licenseType.description: The license type to use in OS adaptation.

networkTags.description: A list of network tags to associate with the VM.

vmName.description: The name of the VM to create.

enumDescriptions: An unspecified disk type. Will be used as STANDARD.

extensionCount.description: Output only. The number of times expiration was extended.

expireTime.description: Output only. Timestamp of when this resource is considered expired.

extendable.description: Output only. Describes whether the expiration can be extended.

diskId.description: Optional. Target Compute Engine Disk ID. This is the resource ID segment of the Compute Engine Disk to create. In the resource name compute/v1/projects/{project}/zones/{zone}/disks/disk1 "disk1" is the resource ID for the disk.

zone.description: Required. The Compute Engine zone in which to create the disk. Should be of the form: projects/{target-project}/locations/{zone}

replicaZones.description: Optional. Replication zones of the regional disk. Should be of the form: projects/{target-project}/locations/{replica-zone} Currently only one replica zone is supported.

diskType.description: Required. The disk type to use.
enumDescriptions: An unspecified disk type. Will be used as STANDARD.
A Standard disk type.
SSD hard disk type.
An alternative to SSD persistent disks that balance performance and cost.
Hyperdisk balanced disk type.
Hyperdisk balanced high availability disk type.

CutoverJob.description: CutoverJob message describes a cutover of a migrating VM. The CutoverJob is the operation of shutting down the VM, creating a snapshot and cloning the VM using the replicated snapshot.

stateTime.description: Output only. The time the state was last updated.

name.description: Output only. The name of the cutover job.

progressPercent.description: Output only. The current progress in percentage of the cutover job.

createTime.description: Output only. The time the cutover job was created (as an API call, not when it was actually created in the target).

endTime.description: Output only. The time the cutover job had finished.

computeEngineVmDetails.description: Output only. Details of the VM in Compute Engine. Deprecated: Use compute_engine_target_details instead.

progress.description: Output only. The current progress in percentage of the cutover job.

enumDescriptions: The state is unknown. This is used for API compatibility only and is not used by the system.
The cutover job has not yet started.
The cutover job finished with errors.
The cutover job finished successfully.
The cutover job was cancelled.
The cutover job is being cancelled.
The cutover job is active and running.
OS adaptation is running as part of the cutover job to generate license.
state.description: Output only. State of the cutover job.

targetDetails.description: Output only. Details of the VM to create as the target of this cutover job. Deprecated: Use compute_engine_target_details instead.

stateMessage.description: Output only. A message providing possible extra details about the current state.

steps.description: Output only. The cutover steps list representing its progress.
